   - Add re-export for `jjri_persist` if needed

   352→// ============================================================================
   353→// Public dispatch API
   354→// ============================================================================
   355→
   356→/// Dispatch JJK commands from raw arguments.
   357→///
   358→/// Called by VOK when it receives an external subcommand starting with "jjx_".
   359→/// The first element of `args` should be the subcommand name (e.g., "jjx_muster").
   360→///
   361→/// Returns exit code (0 for success, non-zero for failure).
   362→pub fn jjrx_dispatch(args: &[OsString]) -> i32 {
   363→    // Prepend synthetic binary name for clap parsing.
   364→    // Clap expects args[0] to be the binary name (used in help text).
   365→    // VOK passes ["jjx_muster", ...] so we prepend "jjx" to get ["jjx", "jjx_muster", ...].
   366→    let mut full_args = vec![OsString::from("jjx")];
   367→    full_args.extend(args.iter().cloned());
   368→
   369→    // Parse the subcommand and arguments
   370→    let parsed = match jjrx_JjxCommands::try_parse_from(&full_args) {
   371→        Ok(cmd) => cmd,
   372→        Err(e) => {
   373→            // Let clap handle help/version/error display
   374→            e.print().ok();
   375→            return if e.kind() == clap::error::ErrorKind::DisplayHelp
   376→                   || e.kind() == clap::error::ErrorKind::DisplayVersion {
   377→                0
   378→            } else {
   379→                1
   380→            };
   381→        }
   382→    };
   383→
   384→    match parsed {
   385→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Notch(args) => zjjrx_run_notch(args),
   386→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Chalk(args) => zjjrx_run_chalk(args),
   387→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Rein(args) => zjjrx_run_rein(args),
   388→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Validate(args) => zjjrx_run_validate(args),
   389→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Muster(args) => zjjrx_run_muster(args),
   390→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Saddle(args) => zjjrx_run_saddle(args),
   391→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Parade(args) => zjjrx_run_parade(args),
   392→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Retire(args) => zjjrx_run_retire(args),
   393→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Nominate(args) => zjjrx_run_nominate(args),
   394→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Slate(args) => zjjrx_run_slate(args),
   395→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Rail(args) => zjjrx_run_rail(args),
   396→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Tally(args) => zjjrx_run_tally(args),
   397→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Draft(args) => zjjrx_run_draft(args),
   398→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Furlough(args) => zjjrx_run_furlough(args),
   399→    }
   400→}
   401→
   402→/// Check if a command name is a JJK command
   403→pub fn jjrx_is_jjk_command(name: &str) -> bool {
   404→    name.starts_with("jjx_")
   405→}

   407→// ============================================================================
   408→// Notch/Chalk implementations (use vvc for commits)
   409→// ============================================================================
   410→
   411→fn zjjrx_run_notch(args: jjrx_NotchArgs) -> i32 {
   412→    let coronet = match Coronet::jjrf_parse(&args.coronet) {
   413→        Ok(c) => c,
   414→        Err(e) => {
   415→            eprintln!("jjx_notch: error: {}", e);
   416→            return 1;
   417→        }
   418→    };
   419→
   420→    let prefix = format_notch_prefix(&coronet);
   421→
   422→    let commit_args = vvc::vvcc_CommitArgs {
   423→        prefix: Some(prefix),
   424→        message: None,
   425→        allow_empty: false,
   426→        no_stage: false,
   427→        size_limit: args.size_limit.unwrap_or(vvc::VVCG_SIZE_LIMIT),
   428→        warn_limit: vvc::VVCG_WARN_LIMIT,
   429→    };
   430→
   431→    vvc::commit(&commit_args)
   432→}
   433→
   434→fn zjjrx_run_chalk(args: jjrx_ChalkArgs) -> i32 {
   435→    let marker = match ChalkMarker::jjrn_parse(&args.marker) {
   436→        Ok(m) => m,
   437→        Err(e) => {
   438→            eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   439→            return 1;
   440→        }
   441→    };
   442→
   443→    // Try parsing as Coronet first (5 base64 chars), then as Firemark (2 base64 chars)
   444→    let identity = args.identity.strip_prefix('₢').or_else(|| args.identity.strip_prefix('₣')).unwrap_or(&args.identity);
   445→
   446→    let message = if identity.len() == 5 {
   447→        // Coronet - pace-level chalk
   448→        let coronet = match Coronet::jjrf_parse(&args.identity) {
   449→            Ok(c) => c,
   450→            Err(e) => {
   451→                eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   452→                return 1;
   453→            }
   454→        };
   455→        format_chalk_message(&coronet, marker, &args.description)
   456→    } else if identity.len() == 2 {
   457→        // Firemark - heat-level (discussion only)
   458→        if marker.jjrn_requires_pace() {
   459→            eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {} marker requires a Coronet (pace identity), not a Firemark", marker.jjrn_as_str());
   460→            return 1;
   461→        }
   462→        let firemark = match Firemark::jjrf_parse(&args.identity) {
   463→            Ok(fm) => fm,
   464→            Err(e) => {
   465→                eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   466→                return 1;
   467→            }
   468→        };
   469→        format_heat_discussion(&firemark, &args.description)
   470→    } else {
   471→        eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: identity must be Coronet (5 chars) or Firemark (2 chars), got {} chars", identity.len());
   472→        return 1;
   473→    };
   474→
   475→    let commit_args = vvc::vvcc_CommitArgs {
   476→        prefix: None,
   477→        message: Some(message),
   478→        allow_empty: true,
   479→        no_stage: true,
   480→        size_limit: vvc::VVCG_SIZE_LIMIT,
   481→        warn_limit: vvc::VVCG_WARN_LIMIT,
   482→    };
   483→
   484→    vvc::commit(&commit_args)
   485→}

   491→fn zjjrx_run_rein(args: zjjrx_ReinArgs) -> i32 {

   502→fn zjjrx_run_validate(args: zjjrx_ValidateArgs) -> i32 {
   503→    let gallops = match Gallops::jjrg_load(&args.file) {
   504→        Ok(g) => g,
   505→        Err(e) => {
   506→            eprintln!("jjx_validate: error loading Gallops: {}", e);
   507→            return 1;
   508→        }
   509→    };

   584→fn zjjrx_run_retire(args: zjjrx_RetireArgs) -> i32 {
   585→    use crate::jjrc_core::jjrc_timestamp_date;
   586→    use crate::jjrg_gallops::jjrg_RetireArgs as LibRetireArgs;
   587→    use crate::jjrs_steeplechase::{jjrs_ReinArgs as ReinArgs, jjrs_get_entries as get_entries};
   588→    use std::path::Path;
   589→
   590→    let firemark = match Firemark::jjrf_parse(&args.firemark) {
   591→        Ok(fm) => fm,
   592→        Err(e) => {
   593→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: error: {}", e);
   594→            return 1;
   595→        }
   596→    };
   597→
   598→    // Load gallops
   599→    let gallops = match Gallops::jjrg_load(&args.file) {
   600→        Ok(g) => g,
   601→        Err(e) => {
   602→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: error loading Gallops: {}", e);
   603→            return 1;
   604→        }
   605→    };
   606→
   607→    // Get steeplechase entries (filtered by firemark identity)
   608→    let rein_args = ReinArgs {
   609→        firemark: args.firemark.clone(),
   610→        limit: 1000,
   611→    };
   612→    let steeplechase = match get_entries(&rein_args) {
   613→        Ok(entries) => entries,
   614→        Err(e) => {
   615→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: warning: could not get steeplechase: {}", e);
   616→            Vec::new()
   617→        }
   618→    };
   619→
   620→    // Compute base path from gallops file
   621→    let base_path = args.file.parent()
   622→        .and_then(|p| p.parent())
   623→        .and_then(|p| p.parent())
   624→        .unwrap_or(Path::new("."));
   625→
   626→    // If --execute not specified, output trophy markdown preview
   627→    if !args.execute {
   628→        // Read paddock content
   629→        let firemark_key = firemark.jjrf_display();
   630→        let heat = match gallops.heats.get(&firemark_key) {
   631→            Some(h) => h,
   632→            None => {
   633→                eprintln!("jjx_retire: error: Heat '{}' not found", firemark_key);
   634→                return 1;
   635→            }
   636→        };
   637→        let paddock_path = base_path.join(&heat.paddock_file);
   638→        let paddock_content = match std::fs::read_to_string(&paddock_path) {
   639→            Ok(c) => c,
   640→            Err(e) => {
   641→                eprintln!("jjx_retire: error reading paddock: {}", e);
   642→                return 1;
   643→            }
   644→        };
   645→
   646→        // Build and output trophy preview
   647→        let today = jjrc_timestamp_date();
   648→        match gallops.jjrg_build_trophy_preview(&args.firemark, &paddock_content, &today, &steeplechase) {
   649→            Ok(markdown) => {
   650→                println!("{}", markdown);
   651→                return 0;
   652→            }
   653→            Err(e) => {
   654→                eprintln!("jjx_retire: error: {}", e);
   655→                return 1;
   656→            }
   657→        }
   658→    }
   659→
   660→    // --execute: perform the actual retire operation
   661→
   662→    // Need mutable gallops for execute path
   663→    let mut gallops = gallops;
   664→
   665→    // Acquire lock FIRST
   666→    let lock = match vvc::vvcc_CommitLock::vvcc_acquire() {
   667→        Ok(l) => l,
   668→        Err(e) => {
   669→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: error: {}", e);
   670→            return 1;
   671→        }
   672→    };
   673→
   674→    // Execute retire
   675→    let retire_args = LibRetireArgs {
   676→        firemark: args.firemark.clone(),
   677→        today: jjrc_timestamp_date(),
   678→    };
   679→
   680→    let result = match gallops.jjrg_retire(retire_args, base_path, &steeplechase) {
   681→        Ok(r) => r,
   682→        Err(e) => {
   683→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: error: {}", e);
   684→            return 1;
   685→        }
   686→    };
   687→
   688→    // Save gallops
   689→    if let Err(e) = gallops.jjrg_save(&args.file) {
   690→        eprintln!("jjx_retire: error saving Gallops: {}", e);
   691→        return 1;
   692→    }
   693→
   694→    // Commit using vvcm_commit with explicit file list
   695→    // Files: gallops.json, trophy file (created), paddock file (deleted - git add handles this)
   696→    let gallops_path = args.file.to_string_lossy().to_string();
   697→    let commit_args = vvc::vvcm_CommitArgs {
   698→        files: vec![
   699→            gallops_path,
   700→            result.trophy_path.clone(),
   701→            result.paddock_path.clone(),
   702→        ],
   703→        message: format_heat_message(&firemark, HeatAction::Retire, &result.silks),
   704→        size_limit: 200000,  // 200KB - trophy files can be large
   705→        warn_limit: 100000,
   706→    };
   707→
   708→    match vvc::machine_commit(&lock, &commit_args) {
   709→        Ok(hash) => {
   710→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: committed {}", &hash[..8]);
   711→        }
   712→        Err(e) => {
   713→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: commit warning: {}", e);
   714→        }
   715→    }
   716→
   717→    // Output result
   718→    println!("trophy: {}", result.trophy_path);
   719→    println!("Heat {} retired successfully", result.firemark);
   720→
   721→    0
   722→    // lock released here
   723→}

   891→fn zjjrx_run_rail(args: zjjrx_RailArgs) -> i32 {
   892→    use crate::jjrg_gallops::jjrg_RailArgs as LibRailArgs;
   893→
   894→    // Acquire lock FIRST - fail fast if another operation is in progress
   895→    let lock = match vvc::vvcc_CommitLock::vvcc_acquire() {
   896→        Ok(l) => l,
   897→        Err(e) => {
   898→            eprintln!("jjx_rail: error: {}", e);
   899→            return 1;
   900→        }
   901→    };
   902→
   903→    let order: Vec<String> = if args.order.len() == 1 && args.order[0].starts_with('[') {
   904→        match serde_json::from_str(&args.order[0]) {
   905→            Ok(v) => v,
   906→            Err(_) => args.order.clone(),
   907→        }
   908→    } else {
   909→        args.order.clone()
   910→    };
   911→
   912→    let mut gallops = match Gallops::jjrg_load(&args.file) {
   913→        Ok(g) => g,
   914→        Err(e) => {
   915→            eprintln!("jjx_rail: error loading Gallops: {}", e);
   916→            return 1;
   917→        }
   918→    };
   919→
   920→    let firemark = args.firemark.clone();
   921→    let move_coronet = args.r#move.clone();
   922→    let move_before = args.before.clone();
   923→    let move_after = args.after.clone();
   924→    let move_first = args.first;
   925→    let move_last = args.last;
   926→    let rail_args = LibRailArgs {
   927→        firemark: args.firemark,

   929→        move_coronet: args.r#move,
   930→        before: args.before,

   941→            }

   943→            // Compute descriptive subject for commit message
   944→            let subject = if let Some(ref moved) = move_coronet {
   945→                // Move mode: describe where the pace was moved
   946→                let target = if move_first { "to first".to_string() }
   947→                    else if move_last { "to last".to_string() }
   948→                    else if let Some(ref b) = move_before { format!("before {}", b) }
   949→                    else if let Some(ref a) = move_after { format!("after {}", a) }
   950→                    else { "???".to_string() };
   951→                format!("moved {} {}", moved, target)

   954→                format!("order: {}", new_order.join(", "))
   955→            };
   956→

   968→                warn_limit: 30000,
   969→            };
   970→
   971→            match vvc::machine_commit(&lock, &commit_args) {
   972→                Ok(hash) => {
   973→                    eprintln!("jjx_rail: committed {}", &hash[..8]);
   974→                }
   975→                Err(e) => {
   976→                    eprintln!("jjx_rail: commit warning: {}", e);
   977→                }
   978→            }
   979→
   980→            for coronet in new_order {

   993→fn zjjrx_run_tally(args: zjjrx_TallyArgs) -> i32 {
   994→    use crate::jjrg_gallops::jjrg_TallyArgs as LibTallyArgs;
   995→
   996→    eprintln!("jjx_tally: starting");
   997→
   998→    // Acquire lock FIRST - fail fast if another operation is in progress
   999→    let lock = match vvc::vvcc_CommitLock::vvcc_acquire() {
  1000→        Ok(l) => l,
  1001→        Err(e) => {
  1002→            eprintln!("jjx_tally: error: {}", e);
  1003→            return 1;
  1004→        }
  1005→    };
  1006→    eprintln!("jjx_tally: lock acquired");
  1007→
  1008→    let text = match read_stdin_optional() {
  1009→        Ok(t) => t,
  1010→        Err(e) => {
  1011→            eprintln!("jjx_tally: error: {}", e);
  1012→            return 1;
  1013→        }
  1014→    };
  1015→    eprintln!("jjx_tally: stdin read ({} bytes)", text.as_ref().map(|t| t.len()).unwrap_or(0));
  1016→
  1017→    let state = match &args.state {
  1018→        Some(s) => match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {
  1019→            "rough" => Some(PaceState::Rough),
  1020→            "bridled" => Some(PaceState::Bridled),
  1021→            "complete" => Some(PaceState::Complete),
  1022→            "abandoned" => Some(PaceState::Abandoned),
  1023→            _ => {
  1024→                eprintln!("jjx_tally: error: invalid state '{}', must be rough, bridled, complete, or abandoned", s);
  1025→                return 1;
  1026→            }
  1027→        },
  1028→        None => None,
  1029→    };
  1030→
  1031→    let mut gallops = match Gallops::jjrg_load(&args.file) {
  1032→        Ok(g) => g,
  1033→        Err(e) => {
  1034→            eprintln!("jjx_tally: error loading Gallops: {}", e);
  1035→            return 1;
  1036→        }
  1037→    };
  1038→    eprintln!("jjx_tally: gallops loaded");
  1039→
  1040→    // Get firemark and silks for commit message before we move args
  1041→    let coronet_str = args.coronet.clone();
  1042→    let (fm, silks) = match Coronet::jjrf_parse(&coronet_str) {
  1043→        Ok(c) => {
  1044→            let parent_fm = c.jjrf_parent_firemark();
  1045→            let silks = gallops.heats.get(&parent_fm.jjrf_display())
  1046→                .and_then(|h| h.paces.get(&c.jjrf_display()))
  1047→                .and_then(|p| p.tacks.first().map(|t| t.silks.clone()))
  1048→                .unwrap_or_else(|| coronet_str.clone());
  1049→            (parent_fm, silks)
  1050→        }
  1051→        Err(e) => {
  1052→            eprintln!("jjx_tally: error: {}", e);
  1053→            return 1;
  1054→        }
  1055→    };
  1056→
  1057→    let tally_args = LibTallyArgs {
  1058→        coronet: args.coronet,
  1059→        state,
  1060→        direction: args.direction,
  1061→        text,
  1062→        silks: args.silks,
  1063→    };
  1064→
  1065→    eprintln!("jjx_tally: calling jjrg_tally");
  1066→    match gallops.jjrg_tally(tally_args) {
  1067→        Ok(()) => {
  1068→            eprintln!("jjx_tally: tally succeeded, saving gallops");
  1069→            if let Err(e) = gallops.jjrg_save(&args.file) {
  1070→                eprintln!("jjx_tally: error saving Gallops: {}", e);
  1071→                return 1;
  1072→            }

### Key Pattern

The refactoring establishes a consistent pattern for JJK operations that modify gallops:
1. Acquire lock first (fail fast)
2. Load gallops
3. Perform the operation (nominate/slate/rail/tally/furlough)
4. Call `jjri_persist` to save and commit in one atomic operation
5. Handle the result and print output

This reduces code duplication and ensures all operations follow the same save-commit discipline.
